passages.explanation: Jesus looks up to heaven and prays aloud. This entire chapter is a prayer — the longest recorded prayer of Jesus in the gospels. He asks the Father to glorify him so that he can glorify the Father in return. The Father gave him authority over all people so he could give eternal life to those given to him. He defines eternal life: knowing the only true God and Jesus Christ whom God sent. He says he has completed the work the Father gave him on earth, and asks to be restored to the glory he shared with the Father before the world existed.

Jesus prays specifically for his disciples. He revealed the Father's name to them, and they have obeyed the Father's word. They know that everything Jesus has comes from the Father. They received his words and believe he was sent by God. He prays for them — not for the world at this moment, but for these specific people the Father gave him. He's about to leave the world, but they'll remain in it. He asks the Father to protect them and keep them unified. While Jesus was with them, he kept them safe — none were lost except Judas, fulfilling Scripture. He asks the Father not to take them out of the world but to protect them from the evil one. They don't belong to the world, just as Jesus doesn't. He asks the Father to sanctify them — set them apart — through truth. Just as the Father sent Jesus into the world, Jesus is sending them into the world.

Jesus expands his prayer beyond the disciples to all future believers — everyone who will believe through their testimony. He prays that all of them will be united, just as the Father and Son are united, so that the world will believe God sent him. The glory the Father gave Jesus, Jesus has given to them, so they can be one. He wants them to be with him and see his glory — the glory the Father gave him because he loved him before the world was created. He closes by saying the world doesn't know the Father, but Jesus knows him, and the disciples know that the Father sent Jesus. He has made the Father's name known to them and will continue to do so, so that the Father's love for Jesus will be in them, and Jesus himself will be in them.
